text: Objective Blockchain is a new technological revolution, which provides an innovative solution to the many health challenges such as interoperability, privacy, management of data and resources, etc. This review aimed at identifying the applications of Blockchain in health sector with special focus on the use cases, where the technology is successfully implemented and tested in a trial environment. We adhered to PRISMA guidelines and conducted this systematic review by searching academic publications, which have implemented real-world trials on Blockchain technology in the health sector. Our search was on top-rated scientific sources like Scopus, PubMed, ScienceDirect, etc., and after filtering 723 scientific studies, our findings reduced to 10 high-quality studies. The reviews of these studies reveal international interest in the development and applications of Blockchain in health sector across the globe and specifically in countries like United Arab Emirates, China and India. It further reveals the applications of Blockchain technology in various aspects of the health sector such as securing Electronic Health Records (EHRs), integrity in pharmaceutical supply chains, managing medical waste, as well as in the donation of blood. The studies relating to the experimental trials had designed all the aspects of blockchain technology in the health sector and the trials gave promising results. The data after decryption was effectively stored through the smart contracts and the data was highly secure due to the decentralized network given by the Blockchain technology. Conclusion Although blockchain technology has many benefits for healthcare sector such as providing information of health sector transparently, preventing fraud and many more, its adoption for the healthcare sector is on a trial basis as yet due to limitations and need of improvements in the performance of Blockchain technology, in the aspects of future scalability of the network, and the formulation of governing policies.
explanation: The text is quite generic with broad, non-specific claims and some awkward phrasing, which can be indicative of AI-generated summaries, though a careful human abstract could resemble this style.
